title: Hiraea mortoniana
intro: Hiraea mortoniana, also known as Morton's hiraea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Hiraea mortoniana include iridoids and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Hiraea mortoniana is classified within the family Malpighiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Hiraea, which consists of various species of flowering plants. The family Malpighiaceae is a part of the larger clade Asterids, which is one of the two main groups of flowering plants. Malpighiaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as Barbados or Malpighia. They are native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.
